codeQuality.notes: Clean, well-organized code that follows all established codebase conventions (Zustand devtools pattern, object literal service export, Deno edge function with shared helpers). Good section headers, clear type definitions, and proper use of @/ imports. Minor deductions for: unused resolution parameter in captureTake, setWorldGenerating no-op without documentation, and limited service-level test coverage (only getImageSize tested, no supabase mock tests).

codeReview.summary: Solid foundation implementation. All 4 files (types, store, service, edge function) are created with correct structure and follow project patterns precisely. The 24 tests pass covering core store actions and service helpers. The implementation fully satisfies the feature description and all expectedBehavior items. A few non-blocking issues were found.

issues.description: captureTake accepts a 'resolution: ResolutionType' parameter but never uses it. The canvas is captured at its native size. This unused parameter may confuse consumers expecting resolution-based resizing. Either use the parameter to resize the canvas output, or remove it from the signature and document that resolution should be set on the canvas before capture.

setWorldGenerating is declared in the WorldviewActions interface but implemented as a no-op with a comment. While the rationale is documented in the inline comment (tracked externally via WorldOperation), having a no-op method in the public API is potentially misleading. Consider either tracking a 'generating' flag per-world in the store, or marking this as a placeholder in the interface with a TODO.

Error responses forward raw WorldLabs API error text to the client (e.g., 'WorldLabs generate failed: <raw text>'). While this is consistent with the existing fal-proxy pattern, the _shared/response.ts exports a safeErrorResponse() helper that masks internal details. For production use, consider using safeErrorResponse for unexpected errors while keeping specific validation errors (missing params) user-facing.

Service tests only cover getImageSize (pure function) and export signature checks. No mocked tests verify that generateWorld, pollOperation, captureTake, etc. correctly call supabase.functions.invoke with the right arguments. The feature spec says 'Tests written for store actions and service functions' — service function behavior beyond signatures is not tested.

pollOperation has no cancellation mechanism (no AbortController support). If a component unmounts while polling, the 10-minute polling loop will continue silently in the background. For a foundation layer this is acceptable, but consumers will need to handle cleanup.
captureTake accepts 'resolution' parameter but never uses it — unused function parameter.
setWorldGenerating is a no-op — declared in interface but does nothing. May mislead consumers.
Raw WorldLabs error text forwarded to clients. Consistent with fal-proxy pattern but safeErrorResponse exists.
Service method behavior not tested beyond signatures — no mocked supabase.functions.invoke tests.
pollOperation lacks AbortController support for cancellation on component unmount.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure step 3 says 'Write Tests First (Red Phase)' but worker correctly noted this is impractical for greenfield foundation features where types and store don't exist yet. The skill should document an exception for foundation/greenfield features where implementation-first with tests-after is acceptable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The skill says Write Tests First but for a foundation feature creating new files from scratch, writing tests before the types/store exist would require constant refactoring.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The setWorldGenerating no-op pattern (interface method that intentionally does nothing because state is tracked externally) is not documented as a convention. If this is an intentional architectural pattern, it should be documented to avoid future confusion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/lib/stores/worldview-store.ts:167-170 — setWorldGenerating is a no-op with inline comment.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The WorldLabs Marble API endpoints and auth header format (WLT-Api-Key, not Bearer) are documented in AGENTS.md's API Reference section. However, the actual response shapes from WorldLabs (operation object structure, world object structure, upload URL format) are not documented and were inferred by the worker. Adding response schemas to the library would help future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worldlabs-proxy/index.ts returns raw WorldLabs responses as-is — the client-side code casts them (e.g., 'data as WorldOperation', 'data as World') assuming specific shapes.

summary: PASS — The worldview-foundation feature is a clean, well-structured implementation that creates all 4 foundational files (types, store, service, edge function) plus 24 passing tests. Code follows all established project conventions: Zustand with devtools, object literal service pattern, Deno edge function with shared auth/response helpers, @/ import aliases. All expectedBehavior items from the spec are satisfied. Five non-blocking issues found: unused resolution parameter in captureTake, no-op setWorldGenerating, raw error forwarding in edge function, limited service test coverage, and no polling cancellation support. None are blocking — the foundation layer is solid and ready for UI integration.
